name: Text Justification
description: Given an array of strings words and a width maxWidth, format the text such that each line has exactly maxWidth characters and is fully (left and right) justified. Pack as many words as you can in each line. Pad extra spaces when necessary. Extra spaces between words should be distributed as evenly as possible. The last line should be left-justified.
signature: def full_justify(words: list[str], max_width: int) -> list[str]:
tests:
def test_uneven_spaces():
    result = full_justify(['a', 'b', 'c', 'd'], 5)
    # 'a b c' fits (5 chars), 'd' on last line
    assert len(result[-1]) == 5
    # Every line must be exactly max_width
    for line in result:
        assert len(line) == 5

def test_last_line_left_justified():
    result = full_justify(['the', 'quick', 'brown', 'fox'], 16)
    # Last line should be left-justified with trailing spaces
    last = result[-1]
    assert len(last) == 16
    assert last == last.rstrip() + ' ' * (16 - len(last.rstrip()))
constraints: words[i] consists of only English letters and symbols
